rules.name: Conventional commit format with Co-Authored-By trailer
rules.evidence: conventional commits (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`chore:`) with a `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer when written via Claude Code.

rules.name: CHANGELOG dual-view structure (edit)
rules.evidence: Every release in CHANGELOG.md leads with 🌱 In plain English (anyone can follow it) then 🔧 Technical notes (entity/code detail). Keep both when adding a release.

rules.name: CHANGELOG dual-view structure (write)
rules.evidence: Every release in CHANGELOG.md leads with 🌱 In plain English (anyone can follow it) then 🔧 Technical notes (entity/code detail). Keep both when adding a release.

rules.name: State field additions must preserve old-install tolerance
rules.evidence: Adding a state field → add it to `_fresh_zone` with a safe default and read it with `.get(k)` / `is not None`; never assume it exists in an old file.

rules.name: Add-on option additions must have neutral defaults
rules.evidence: Never require the operator to wipe state, re-run setup, reconfigure, or hand-migrate a schema.

rules.name: Avoid renaming or removing entities old installs rely on
rules.evidence: Renaming/removing an entity an old install relies on is breaking; avoid or migrate.

rules.name: No hardcoded F2-specific entity ids (edit)
rules.evidence: F2-specific values are defaults/overrides, never hardcoded assumptions... A change that only works because of F2's exact names or numbers is a bug.

rules.name: No hardcoded F2-specific entity ids (write)
rules.evidence: F2-specific values are defaults/overrides, never hardcoded assumptions... A change that only works because of F2's exact names or numbers is a bug.

rules.name: State/options/entity changes need a backward-compat test
rules.evidence: A change to state/options/entities isn't done until a test shows an old install still loads.

skill_candidates.summary: Deploy engine/integration changes to the live HA box: copy modules into the add-on and Rebuild via Supervisor (a plain Restart does not pick up code changes); reload custom components separately for integration changes.
skill_candidates.evidence: copy changed modules to `addons/f2_control/` on the live box, then **Rebuild** the add-on from Supervisor (⋮ → Rebuild) — the Dockerfile `COPY f2_control /app` bakes the code into the image at build time, so a plain **restart reuses the old image and does NOT pick up code changes** ... Integration changes: Developer Tools → YAML → Reload Custom Components. For `crop-steering-engine` package changes, update the package source and Rebuild the add-on.

skill_candidates.summary: Verify a shot-sizing change actually took effect by reading the live pump's run-time history, not by trusting that files were copied.
skill_candidates.evidence: pull `switch.veg_main_pump` history (`/api/history/period`) and read the on-period seconds. File-copied + md5-matched proves nothing about the running container.

always_on: Two-layer architecture overview: HA integration (never touches hardware) vs f2-control add-on (live coordinator gated by input_boolean.f2_control_enabled).
Dev commands catalog (ruff/black/yamllint, pytest suites) is documentation only; black is scoped and exempts the add-on from reformatting; PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 workaround note.
Critical files list: config_flow.py, entity platform files, calculations.py, const.py, controller.py, core.py.
Phase logic P0-P3 definitions plus grow-day reset timing, lights-off forcing P3, and dryback-from-peak semantics.
Hardware control sequence (safety checks -> pump prime -> mainline -> zone valve -> irrigate -> shutdown) with valve-close read-back verification.
Key entity naming patterns (global/per-zone/sensor/service naming; engine reads by entity_id, not friendly-name).
Dependencies overview: integration is pure HA + voluptuous, engine is pure Python with no scipy/numpy, add-on installs its own requirements.txt.
Testing suites overview: see TESTING.md, run bash tests/run_ci.sh, suite list including state-migration and version-consistency tests.
Branching model: one active branch (main); retired branches kept as archive/* tags.
Compatibility opening principle: every change must upgrade live installs transparently in place and work zero-setup fresh, without tailoring to one facility.
State/options storage facts: state.json vs options.json locations, atomic writes (tmp + os.replace), no database.
Fresh install mandatory: no manual DB/schema step, sane defaults out of the box.
v0.8.0 historical resolution note: feed_ec_sensor/feed_ph_sensor now optional with empty defaults, and F2 must set them explicitly in its add-on Configuration.
Deploy = Rebuild, never Restart caution: no API rebuild path exists, and an engine change should never be claimed live off a restart.
Shot sizing formula and unit caution: dur = shot% x substrate_l / flow_lps, entered as per-plant block scaled by plant_count.
F2 facts: 3 zones x 36 plants, 6 L block/plant, 4 L/hr drippers, lights 10:00-22:00, feed gate EC 2.3-3.5 / pH 5.8-6.2.
Historical note: retired 'intelligence' layer (RootSense/ClimateSense) recoverable from the archive/pre-doc-cleanup-2026-06 tag.
